source: PTE Predictive Text Embedding through Large-scale Heterogeneous Text Networks

concepts.finding: Heterogeneous text network embedding: constructs bipartite networks of word-word, word-document, and word-label. Learns embeddings via LINE (first-order + second-order proximity) over this heterogeneous graph. Preserves both local and global structure.
concepts.implication: Keyword clustering can benefit from heterogeneous network construction: keyword-keyword co-occurrence, keyword-document, keyword-intent label relations. This captures richer semantic signal than just keyword co-occurrence.
concepts.open_question: What node types (keywords, documents, intents, categories) should form the heterogeneous network for keyword clustering?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A heterogeneous embedding approach (PTE-style) for keywords, documents, and intent labels will outperform homogeneous keyword-only embeddings for intent clustering.
